title: Chemistry Basics
text: The smallest unit of an element that retains its chemical properties is a {{blank-1}}. A substance formed by the chemical combination of two or more elements is called a {{blank-2}}. The number of {{blank-3}} in an atom determines its chemical properties. The positively charged center of an atom is the {{blank-4}}. Negatively charged particles that orbit the nucleus are called {{blank-5}}.
